vibe: Tactical Minimalism, high-contrast, bold, premium SaaS OS for gym operations.

headings.rule: Avoid Inter/Roboto for headings. We need a heavy-weight athletic feel.

body.rule: Use geometric sans for maximum legibility of data and dashboards.

spacing_and_layout.philosophy: Generous Spacing: 2-3x more spacing than typical templates.
spacing_and_layout.dashboard_grid: Mode B: High Density Grid. grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-4. gap-4 or gap-6. Every pixel utilized. h-full on children.
spacing_and_layout.cards: Cardless or sharp edges (max 4px radius). Use 1px solid strokes (border-white/10). No soft shadows.

components.surfaces: Solid flat backgrounds. NO complete transparent backgrounds in headers or overlays. Use Glassmorphism (backdrop-blur-xl bg-black/60) or solid colors.
components.buttons: Sharp buttons or slight radius (4px). Explicit hover states (hover:bg-[#FF5C53] hover:text-white).
components.forms: Use Shadcn UI (input, select, checkbox). Strip Vercel-look. Darker inputs (bg-[#141414] border-[#262626]). Focus must be high contrast.
components.charts: Shadcn charts or Recharts. Bright thin lines, dark tooltips, no heavy light backgrounds.

visual_enhancers.motion: Hover states on all interactive elements. -translate-y-1. DO NOT use 'transition: all'. Apply to specific properties.
visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle noise overlay or pure matte finishes. No glossy gradients.
visual_enhancers.borders: Expose the grid: border-r border-b border-border/40.

login_hero.description: Dark atmospheric gym for auth screen background

dashboard_banner.description: Athletic man working out - use for promotional or membership cards

media.rules: Never use placeholder images. Always use provided URLs or colored initials.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure WCAG AA contrast. 4.5:1 for normal text.
accessibility.focus: High contrast focus rings. focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[#FF3B30] focus:ring-offset-2 focus:ring-offset-black.

instructions_to_main_agent: Use .js files for React components, not .tsx files.
Use 'lucide-react' for standard icons. Install if needed.
All interactive elements (buttons, inputs, links, list items) MUST include a 'data-testid' attribute in kebab-case format (e.g., data-testid='login-submit-button').
For the gym operating system, focus on dense, actionable dashboard modules: Member Management, Attendence Heatmaps, Trainer Rosters.
Never use 'text-align: center' globally on the App.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur).
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style.
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN.
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable.
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed.
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute to facilitate robust automated testing.
